content.content:
280	  /**
281	   * Optional static-file serving (production single-binary mode). When
282	   * set, mounts `@ax/static-files` after the API routes so it serves
283	   * channel-web's bundle on otherwise-unmatched paths. SPA fallback
284	   * defaults to `true` (returns `index.html` for unknown paths so
285	   * client-side routing works). Leave unset for dev ã Vite handles
286	   * static + proxy in dev.
287	   */
288	  staticFiles?: {
289	    /** Absolute path to the directory to serve (e.g., channel-web/dist). */
290	    dir: string;
291	    /** URL pattern, defaults to `/*`. */
292	    mountPath?: string;
293	    /**
294	     * SPA fallback (default `true` = serve `index.html` on miss).
295	     * Pass a string to use a different fallback file; pass `false`
296	     * to return 404 on miss.
297	     */
298	    spaFallback?: boolean | string;
299	  };
300	}
301	
302	/**
303	 * Build the production plugin list for k8s mode. Pass the result straight to
304	 * `bootstrap({ bus, plugins, config: {} })` from @ax/core.
305	 *
306	 * Note: the runner binary resolution relies on runtime environment (the
307	 * lookup walks node_modules). That's intentional ã resolved paths are not
308	 * in the JSON config schema. Anthropic credentials live in the credentials
309	 * store (admin-seeded via /admin/credentials, Phase 9.5) and are resolved
310	 * by the credential-proxy at proxy:open-session time, not at plugin init.
311	 */
312	export function createK8sPlugins(config: K8sPresetConfig): Plugin[] {
313	  const plugins: Plugin[] = [];
314	
315	  // ----- 1. stateful base layer ------------------------------------------
316	  // database-postgres owns the shared pg.Pool. storage-postgres reaches it
317	  // via `database:get-instance` (not a direct import ã invariant I2).
318	  plugins.push(
319	    createDatabasePostgresPlugin({
320	      connectionString: config.database.connectionString,
321	      ...(config.database.poolMax !== undefined
322	        ? { poolMax: config.database.poolMax }
323	        : {}),
324	    }),
325	  );
326	  plugins.push(createStoragePostgresPlugin());
327	
328	  // Credentials: facade over the storage-blob seam. The default backend
329	  // (@ax/credentials-store-db) wraps storage:get/storage:set with the
330	  // `credential:` key prefix; the facade owns AES-256-GCM and the
331	  // consumer-facing credentials:get/set/delete contract. Init throws if
332	  // AX_CREDENTIALS_KEY isn't in env.
333	  plugins.push(createCredentialsStoreDbPlugin());
334	  plugins.push(createCredentialsPlugin());
335	
336	  // Phase 3 ã Anthropic OAuth per-kind sub-services. Same load reasoning
337	  // as the CLI preset: purely additive, only dispatches when an agent
338	  // actually carries a kind: 'anthropic-oauth' credential. Web-chat OAuth
339	  // routes (Phase 10ã12) will register HTTP handlers from this plugin.
340	  plugins.push(createCredentialsAnthropicOauthPlugin());
341	
342	  // Phase 2 ã credential-proxy on a Unix socket. The host pod mounts an
343	  // emptyDir at /var/run/ax (helm template); the proxy listens on
344	  // <mount>/proxy.sock. Sandbox pods get the SAME emptyDir mounted via
345	  // the pod template, so they can dial the socket directly. The runner-
346	  // side @ax/credential-proxy-bridge converts that to a loopback TCP
347	  // port inside the sandbox before HTTP(S)_PROXY-aware libraries reach
348	  // it. (Off-the-shelf libraries can't dial a Unix socket directly.)
349	  //
350	  // Real Anthropic credentials are seeded by the admin via the standard
351	  // credentials store (POST /admin/credentials, Phase 9.5); the proxy
352	  // resolves them at proxy:open-session time and substitutes the
353	  // ax-cred:<hex> placeholder into outbound headers mid-flight.
354	  const credentialProxyCfg: Parameters<typeof createCredentialProxyPlugin>[0] = {
355	    listen: {
356	      kind: 'unix',
357	      path: config.credentialProxy?.socketPath ?? '/var/run/ax/proxy.sock',
358	    },
359	  };
360	  if (config.credentialProxy?.caDir !== undefined) {
361	    credentialProxyCfg.caDir = config.credentialProxy.caDir;
362	  }
363	  plugins.push(createCredentialProxyPlugin(credentialProxyCfg));
364	
365	  // ----- 2. cross-process coordination ----------------------------------
366	  // Eventbus and session each own a dedicated LISTEN client.
367	  plugins.push(
368	    createEventbusPostgresPlugin({
369	      connectionString: config.eventbus.connectionString,
370	    }),
371	  );
372	  plugins.push(
373	    createSessionPostgresPlugin({
374	      connectionString: config.session.connectionString,
375	      ...(config.session.poolMax !== undefined
376	        ? { poolMax: config.session.poolMax }
377	        : {}),
378	    }),
379	  );
380	
381	  // ----- 3. workspace ----------------------------------------------------
382	  // Discriminated on `backend`. The shape difference is intentional: `local`
383	  // wants a filesystem path; `http` and `git-protocol` both want a URL +
384	  // bearer token but route to different storage tiers. All three register
385	  // the same four `workspace:*` service hooks (Invariant I1 ã the contract
386	  // is the same regardless of backend).
387	  if (config.workspace.backend === 'git-protocol') {
388	    plugins.push(
389	      createWorkspaceGitServerPlugin({
390	        baseUrl: config.workspace.baseUrl,
391	        token: config.workspace.token,
392	      }),
393	    );
394	  } else if (config.workspace.backend === 'http') {
395	    plugins.push(
396	      createWorkspaceGitHttpPlugin({
397	        baseUrl: config.workspace.baseUrl,
398	        token: config.workspace.token,
399	      }),
400	    );
401	  } else {
402	    plugins.push(
403	      createWorkspaceGitPlugin({
404	        repoRoot: config.workspace.repoRoot,
405	      }),
406	    );
407	  }
408	
409	  // ----- 4. audit log ----------------------------------------------------
410	  // Subscribes to event.http-egress and writes one row per egress via
411	  // storage:set. Push order isn't load-bearing ã the kernel finishes init
412	  // (and therefore wires every subscriber) before any hook fires ã but we
413	  // keep audit-log near the storage layer because that's its only call.
414	  plugins.push(auditLogPlugin());
415	
416	  // Phase 3: validator-skill subscribes to workspace:pre-apply and
417	  // vetoes SKILL.md changes with malformed YAML frontmatter. Push
418	  // order isn't load-bearing (kernel sequences subscribers via
419	  // manifest), but we keep it adjacent to audit-log because both are
420	  // observe-only hook subscribers with no dependencies of their own.
421	  plugins.push(createValidatorSkillPlugin());
422	
423	  // ----- 5. control-plane access (Week 9.5) -----------------------------
424	  // http-server provides the public-facing listener. auth registers the
425	  // /auth/* + /admin/{me,sign-out} routes and the auth:require-user gate
426	  // every admin endpoint depends on. teams owns /admin/teams* and the
427	  // teams:* hooks the agents-plugin's team-visibility ACL calls.
428	  //
429	  // Boot order: the kernel topologically sorts on `manifest.calls`
430	  // (http:register-route ã @ax/http-server, auth:require-user ã @ax/auth),
431	  // so even if we shuffled the array the kernel would init http-server
432	  // before auth before agents/teams. We push in the conceptual order to
433	  // keep the intent legible to readers.
434	  plugins.push(
435	    createHttpServerPlugin({
436	      host: config.http.host,
437	      port: config.http.port,
438	      cookieKey: parseCookieKeyString(config.http.cookieKey),
439	      allowedOrigins: config.http.allowedOrigins,
440	    }),
441	  );
442	
443	  const authConfig: AuthConfig = { providers: {} };
444	  if (config.auth.google !== undefined) {
445	    authConfig.providers.google = {
446	      clientId: config.auth.google.clientId,
447	      clientSecret: config.auth.google.clientSecret,
448	      issuer: config.auth.google.issuer,
449	      redirectUri: config.auth.google.redirectUri,
450	    };
451	  }
452	  if (config.auth.devBootstrap !== undefined) {
453	    authConfig.devBootstrap = { token: config.auth.devBootstrap.token };
454	  }
455	  if (config.auth.sessionLifetimeSeconds !== undefined) {
456	    authConfig.sessionLifetimeSeconds = config.auth.sessionLifetimeSeconds;
457	  }
458	  plugins.push(createAuthPlugin(authConfig));
459	
460	  // teams: mountAdminRoutes:true so /admin/teams* lands alongside the rest
461	  // of the admin surface. The plugin's manifest expands `calls` to include
462	  // http:register-route + auth:require-user when this flag is set, which
463	  // is exactly what the kernel's topo-sort needs to see.
464	  plugins.push(createTeamsPlugin({ mountAdminRoutes: true }));
465	
466	  // static-files (optional): serves channel-web's bundle from the same
467	  // listener so cookies and CSRF stay same-origin in production. The
468	  // plugin registers a `/*` catchall LAST so every API route registered
469	  // earlier wins. When `staticFiles` is unset, no catchall is mounted
470	  // and unknown paths return 404 ã the dev workflow uses Vite's proxy
471	  // instead. SPA fallback defaults on so client-side routing works.
472	  if (config.staticFiles !== undefined) {
473	    plugins.push(
474	      createStaticFilesPlugin({
475	        dir: config.staticFiles.dir,
476	        ...(config.staticFiles.mountPath !== undefined
477	          ? { mountPath: config.staticFiles.mountPath }
478	          : {}),
479	        spaFallback: config.staticFiles.spaFallback ?? true,

content.subject: Task 1: Add conversations:set-title hook
content.description: Add `conversations:set-title` service hook to @ax/conversations with `ifNull` semantics (atomic UPDATE WHERE title IS NULL). See plan ôÏ"Task 1" in docs/plans/2026-05-03-runner-owned-sessions-phase-f-impl.md. Includes: SetTitleInput/Output types, store.setTitle(), plugin manifest update, ACL gate via agents:resolve, full test suite (acl, idempotent, validation, ifNull semantic, not-found, soft-deleted).

content.subject: Task 2: Scaffold @ax/llm-anthropic package
content.description: Scaffold packages/llm-anthropic with package.json, tsconfig.json, vitest.config.ts, SECURITY.md, src/index.ts placeholder. Add @anthropic-ai/sdk dep (match runner version), zod. Mirror @ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner-host shape. Add tsconfig reference.

content.subject: Task 3: Implement llm:call registrar
content.description: Implement @ax/llm-anthropic plugin with llm:call registrar. Includes translate.ts (core ã Anthropic SDK shape), plugin.ts (init reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, registers llm:call, retry on 429/5xx), exports createLlmAnthropicPlugin. Verify LlmCallInput/LlmCallOutput types in @ax/core; add if missing. Defaults: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001, maxTokens 4096. Tests: translate shape, plugin init failure, llm:call invocation with stub client, retry logic, 4xx no retry.

content.subject: Task 4: Scaffold @ax/conversation-titles + pure helpers
content.description: Scaffold packages/conversation-titles. Implement pure helpers: prompt.ts (buildPrompt with text/tool-use/tool-result flattening, drops thinking, 4000-char budget), validate.ts (validateGeneratedTitle: trim, strip outer quotes, drop "Untitled", first line only, truncate to TITLE_MAX). Tests for prompt + validate. NO subscriber yet (Task 5). Conversion-titles MUST NOT import @ax/llm-anthropic (I2). Verify whether types-only imports from @ax/conversations are allowed; if not, mirror types locally.

content.subject: Task 5: chat:turn-end subscriber
content.description: Implement @ax/conversation-titles plugin: subscribes chat:turn-end, calls conversations:get + llm:call + conversations:set-title (with ifNull: true). Manifest declares calls + subscribes. Subscriber MUST NOT throw. Skip if role !== 'assistant', conversationId undefined, title already set, transcript empty, validation rejects, llm:call fails. Tests: full e2e with mocked bus + plugin loading.

content.subject: Task 6: Wire into k8s preset
content.description: Modify presets/k8s/src/preset.ts to load createLlmAnthropicPlugin + createConversationTitlesPlugin. Add deps in presets/k8s/package.json. Add tsconfig references. Decide: load unconditionally if ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in canary, else gate behind cfg.titlesEnabled flag. Verify pnpm build + preset-k8s tests pass (no regression).

content.subject: Task 7: Canary acceptance test
content.description: Add a sibling it() in presets/k8s/src/__tests__/acceptance.test.ts: boot preset ã agent:invoke ã poll conversations:get-metadata for non-null title (timeout 30s). Use llm-anthropic clientFactory config to inject deterministic stub client returning "Test Conversation Title". Assert persisted title equals validated stub output.

Mirror format from prior phase PR notes (look at docs/plans/2026-04-29-phase-b-pr-notes.md or similar).

input.prompt:
You are implementing **Tasks 6 + 7** of the Phase F implementation plan in the ax-next repo. Plan: `docs/plans/2026-05-03-runner-owned-sessions-phase-f-impl.md`. Workspace: `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.worktrees/phase-f-conversation-titles`. Branch: `feat/phase-f-conversation-titles`. Tasks 1ã5 already on the branch.

## Important context ã preset wiring scope adjustment

The plan's Task 6 originally said "modify createK8sPlugins() to load both new plugins." **That's wrong** for the current state of the codebase: I verified that `createK8sPlugins()` does NOT load `@ax/conversations` (or `@ax/channel-web`). Loading `@ax/conversation-titles` in `createK8sPlugins()` without conversations would break the kernel's topo-sort (manifest declares `calls: ['conversations:get', 'conversations:set-title']`).

The conversations plugin is loaded **manually** by the existing Phase D canary test in `presets/k8s/src/__tests__/acceptance.test.ts` (around lines 1115ã1130), where both real conversations + permissive agents-stub + storage are layered onto the preset's filtered output.

**Adjusted scope for Tasks 6 + 7:**

- **Task 6:** add the two new packages to `presets/k8s/package.json` `devDependencies` (matching how `@ax/conversations` is currently a devDep). Add tsconfig references in `presets/k8s/tsconfig.json` if needed. Do NOT modify `createK8sPlugins()`. Single tiny commit.
- **Task 7:** add a new sibling `it()` to `presets/k8s/src/__tests__/acceptance.test.ts` mirroring the Phase D canary's pattern (postgres testcontainer + conversations + sqlite storage + sandbox-subprocess + test-proxy + permissive-agents-stub + ...) but with two additions:
  1. `createLlmAnthropicPlugin({ apiKey: 'stub', clientFactory: () => stubClient })` where the stub returns a deterministic `{ text: 'Test Conversation Title', stop_reason: 'end_turn', usage: { input_tokens: 5, output_tokens: 3 } }` for `messages.create()`.
  2. `createConversationTitlesPlugin()` to wire the subscriber.
- The test fires a `chat:turn-end` event with `role: 'assistant'` and the conversationId on the AgentContext, then polls `conversations:get-metadata` for up to 5 seconds checking `title !== null`. Verifies the persisted title is `'Test Conversation Title'`.

## Task 6: Add devDeps + tsconfig refs

### Files to modify
- `presets/k8s/package.json`: add to `devDependencies`:
  - `"@ax/llm-anthropic": "workspace:*"`
  - `"@ax/conversation-titles": "workspace:*"`
  - Place alphabetically among the other `@ax/*` devDeps (the current devDeps are roughly alphabetic ã verify by reading the file).
- `presets/k8s/tsconfig.json`: add tsconfig references if the existing tsconfig has references to other devDep packages (e.g. `@ax/conversations`). If references only exist for prod deps, skip. Read the file first.

### Verify Task 6
- `pnpm install` succeeds.
- `pnpm --filter @ax/preset-k8s build` succeeds.
- `pnpm --filter @ax/preset-k8s test` ã existing tests still pass (no behavior change).

### Commit Task 6

```
chore(preset-k8s): add llm-anthropic + conversation-titles as devDeps

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

## Task 7: Canary acceptance test for auto-titling

### File to modify
- `presets/k8s/src/__tests__/acceptance.test.ts`

### Approach

Find the existing Phase D canary at the bottom of the file (the `it('Phase D canary: conversations:get reads transcript from workspace jsonl', ...)` block, around line 1052). Add a NEW sibling `it()` after it. **Do not modify the Phase D canary** ã leave it intact.

The new test should:

1. Boot postgres testcontainer (via `ensurePostgresStarted()` ã already exists).
2. Build the same plugin list as the Phase D canary (filtered preset + replacements). The new test needs the conversations plugin loaded since `chat:turn-end` triggers `conversations:get` then `conversations:set-title`.
3. **Add two more replacements**:
   - `createLlmAnthropicPlugin({ apiKey: 'stub-key', clientFactory: () => makeStubClient() })`. The stub client's `messages.create()` returns a fixed `{ id, type: 'message', role: 'assistant', content: [{ type: 'text', text: 'Test Conversation Title' }], stop_reason: 'end_turn', usage: { input_tokens: 5, output_tokens: 3 } }`.
   - `createConversationTitlesPlugin()`.
4. Bootstrap the bus.
5. Create a conversation via `conversations:create` (mirroring Phase D's posture).
6. Build an `AgentContext` that includes `conversationId` and `userId` (note: `chat:turn-end` subscribers read `ctx.conversationId` ã verify how Phase D's ctx is built; you may need to use `makeAgentContext({ ..., conversationId: conv.conversationId })` if AgentContext supports that field directly, or set the field on a derived context).
7. **Bind a runner session and seed transcript jsonl** the same way Phase D does ã the titles plugin will call `conversations:get` which reads the workspace jsonl. If transcript is empty, the subscriber returns early without calling `llm:call` (per the plugin's contract). So we need a non-empty transcript.
8. Fire `bus.fire('chat:turn-end', ctx, { role: 'assistant', contentBlocks: [{ type: 'text', text: 'hi back' }], reqId: 'r-canary' })`. Note: `bus.fire` returns a result; await it.
9. **Poll** for the title:
   ```ts
   let title: string | null = null;
   const deadline = Date.now() + 5_000;
   while (Date.now() < deadline) {
     const meta = await bus.call<ConversationsGetMetadataInput, ConversationsGetMetadataOutput>(
       'conversations:get-metadata',
       ctx,
       { conversationId: conv.conversationId, userId },
     );
     if (meta.title !== null) {
       title = meta.title;
       break;
     }
     await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));
   }
   expect(title).toBe('Test Conversation Title');
   ```

All five steps need to work end-to-end.

9. **Capability minimization (CLAUDE.md #5):** the test grants `apiKey: 'stub-key'` to `llm-anthropic`. Is the stub-key value consistent across the codebase (e.g. matches what `plugin.test.ts` uses)? Is there a risk of leaking a real `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` from the test environment?

Important observation: `tsconfig.json` has no project references for `@ax/llm-anthropic` or `@ax/conversation-titles`. The brief said "tsconfig untouched" ã but `src/__tests__` is excluded from the build, so that's intentional. The tests reference only via package devDeps and vitest's runtime resolution. That's consistent with the existing pattern (other test-only deps like `@ax/conversations`, `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner-host`, `@ax/sandbox-subprocess`, etc. are also not in tsconfig refs). OK, principled.

I have enough information to write up the review.

---

## Review: Phase F Tasks 6 + 7

**Files reviewed:**
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.worktrees/phase-f-conversation-titles/presets/k8s/package.json` (Task 6)
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.worktrees/phase-f-conversation-titles/presets/k8s/src/__tests__/acceptance.test.ts` (Task 7, lines 1248ã1502)

**Verification:** `pnpm --filter @ax/preset-k8s test` passes (51/51, including the new Phase F canary). `pnpm --filter @ax/preset-k8s build` succeeds.

### Strengths

- **Canary mirrors its sibling.** The Phase F block parallels the Phase D canary (lines 1056ã1246) almost line-for-line ã same `ensurePostgresStarted()` lazy boot, same git-protocol storage tier setup, same `try/finally` partial-init unwinding pattern, same jsonl seeding shape. A future reader who has internalized Phase D needs zero additional context.
- **Test isolation is clean.** Distinct sqlite filename (`-phasef.sqlite` vs `-phased.sqlite`), distinct `runnerSessionId` UUID, separate `serverRepoRoot` mkdtemp, fresh `tmp` per `beforeEach`. The two postgres-backed canaries can run in either order (or even in parallel under vitest's default thread pool) without cross-contamination.
- **Postgres reuse is principled.** `ensurePostgresStarted()` lazy-boots on first call and reuses the container across canaries ã single ~5s container startup amortized across both Phase D and Phase F.
- **Stub client matches the existing `plugin.test.ts` pattern.** Same `as unknown as Anthropic.Message` cast, same field set (`id/type/role/model/content/stop_reason/stop_sequence/usage`), same nested `usage` shape. The cast is principled given the SDK's discriminated-union types are awkward to construct fully ã the test has chosen to be honest about narrow stubbing rather than performatively thorough.
- **Single-call assertion is load-bearing.** `expect(llmCallCounter.count).toBe(1)` is the I3 (chat:turn-end fires once per turn) witness for the title path. A duplicate-delivery regression elsewhere in the bus would surface here as `count === 2`.
- **`bus.fire` semantics are documented.** Lines 1265ã1269 explicitly note that `bus.fire` awaits subscribers sequentially and that the polling loop is "purely defensive." That's the right framing ã the comment authors know they're belt-and-suspenders. Verified against `hook-bus.ts` line 94: `fire` is `async` and awaits each subscriber's handler in a `for` loop.
- **Strict `!== null` polling matches the spec.** Line 1483: `if (meta.title !== null)`. An empty-string title would not break the loop, would exit at deadline as `null`, and the final `expect(title).toBe('Test Conversation Title')` would surface the bug clearly.
- **CLAUDE.md #3 (no half-wired plugins) is satisfied.** End-to-end chain ã `bus.fire('chat:turn-end')` ã titles subscriber ã `conversations:get` ã `llm:call` ã `conversations:set-title` ã `conversations:get-metadata` ã actually executes (51-test run proves it).
- **Manifest drift safety.** Reuses the existing `PLUGINS_TO_DROP` filter (line 1352) rather than maintaining a parallel list. When the preset adds a new chat-path plugin, this canary picks it up automatically ã same posture as Phase D.

### Issues

No high-confidence (ãË80) issues found.

#### Sub-threshold notes (filtered out, recorded only for context)

These did NOT meet the 80-confidence bar, but listing them so the bar is visible:

- **`@anthropic-ai/sdk` not declared in `presets/k8s/package.json`** ã the test does `import type Anthropic from '@anthropic-ai/sdk'` but only `@ax/llm-anthropic` (which depends on the SDK) is in devDeps. This works under pnpm with hoisting / the existing pnpm-workspace setup, and `pnpm test` confirms it resolves at type-check time. Confidence ~50: this is a type-only import, ergonomic in this monorepo, and the existing pattern doesn't require explicit re-declaration of transitive type-only imports.
- **Stub client omits `container` field** ã SDK 0.91.x's `Anthropic.Message` type may include a `container` field on newer versions; the cast `as unknown as Anthropic.Message` papers over it. Confidence ~40: identical to the pattern in `plugin.test.ts`, and the translator only reads `id/role/type/model/content/stop_reason/usage` (per the comment in `plugin.test.ts` line 8ã9), so missing fields can't surface.
- **Polling loop runs even when `bus.fire` already awaited the subscriber** ã the comment correctly identifies this as defensive. Confidence ~30: documented intent, no actual issue.
- **No assertion that the polled `meta.runnerSessionId` matches what was bound** ã the canary asserts only `title`. Confidence ~25: out of scope for Phase F; Phase D already covers that pathway.

### Final assessment

**Approved.**

Both Task 6 (devDep additions, +2 lines) and Task 7 (canary test, +261 lines) ship a clean, well-isolated end-to-end witness for the Phase F pipeline. The test mirrors its Phase D sibling closely enough that maintenance burden is minimal. The "no half-wired plugins" invariant is satisfied: `@ax/llm-anthropic` and `@ax/conversation-titles` are now reachable from a passing canary.

The only structural choice worth flagging for awareness (not as an issue) is that the canary lives in a `devDependencies`-only test file ã it's never exercised by production preset boot. That's consistent with how the existing Phase D canary is wired (postgres testcontainer + filtered preset, never run in production), and it's the right call given the cost of running an end-to-end k8s + postgres stack in CI for every preset boot. Worth noting only because the Phase F plugins are also currently *not* in the production preset's plugin list ã they're test-only additions. If the intent is for these plugins to ship to production users, that wiring would land in a separate PR (presumably alongside whatever owns the production llm-anthropic API-key sourcing).

   - Does the polling ctx scope `userId` correctly so the metadata read finds the row?

6. **bus.fire await semantics:** does the test await `bus.fire(...)` before polling? If `bus.fire` resolves only after subscribers complete (which the implementer claims by reading hook-bus.ts), is the polling loop redundant ã and if so, is it documented as defensive?

7. **Manifest drift safety:** the existing acceptance test guards against `createK8sPlugins()` adding/removing plugins. Does the Phase F test's plugin layering (filtered preset + replacements) reuse the existing `PLUGINS_TO_DROP` filter or define its own?

8. **No half-wired plugin (CLAUDE.md #3):** the Phase F plugins are now reachable from a canary acceptance test (via Task 7). Confirm the chain works: Plugin loaded ã bus.fire ã subscriber ã bus.call('llm:call') ã bus.call('conversations:set-title') ã metadata read sees the title. All five steps need to work end-to-end.

9. **Capability minimization (CLAUDE.md #5):** the test grants `apiKey: 'stub-key'` to `llm-anthropic`. Is the stub-key value consistent across the codebase (e.g. matches what `plugin.test.ts` uses)? Is there a risk of leaking a real `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` from the test environment?

10. **Test name clarity:** the new test name should communicate what's being verified, not just "Phase F canary." Does the title clearly describe the scenario?

## Run

```bash
git show 55eae5a
git show c19a9b5
pnpm --filter @ax/preset-k8s test
pnpm build
```

## Report

**Strengths:** what's well done?

**Issues (severity-categorized):**
- **Critical:** broken invariants, security, bugs.
- **Important:** maintainability or boundary-review concerns.
- **Minor:** style, doc, dead code.

**Final assessment:** Approved / Needs changes. File:line for every issue.

content.text:
Important observation: `tsconfig.json` has no project references for `@ax/llm-anthropic` or `@ax/conversation-titles`. The brief said "tsconfig untouched" ã but `src/__tests__` is excluded from the build, so that's intentional. The tests reference only via package devDeps and vitest's runtime resolution. That's consistent with the existing pattern (other test-only deps like `@ax/conversations`, `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner-host`, `@ax/sandbox-subprocess`, etc. are also not in tsconfig refs). OK, principled.

I have enough information to write up the review.

---

## Review: Phase F Tasks 6 + 7

**Files reviewed:**
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.worktrees/phase-f-conversation-titles/presets/k8s/package.json` (Task 6)
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.worktrees/phase-f-conversation-titles/presets/k8s/src/__tests__/acceptance.test.ts` (Task 7, lines 1248ã1502)

**Verification:** `pnpm --filter @ax/preset-k8s test` passes (51/51, including the new Phase F canary). `pnpm --filter @ax/preset-k8s build` succeeds.

### Strengths

- **Canary mirrors its sibling.** The Phase F block parallels the Phase D canary (lines 1056ã1246) almost line-for-line ã same `ensurePostgresStarted()` lazy boot, same git-protocol storage tier setup, same `try/finally` partial-init unwinding pattern, same jsonl seeding shape. A future reader who has internalized Phase D needs zero additional context.
- **Test isolation is clean.** Distinct sqlite filename (`-phasef.sqlite` vs `-phased.sqlite`), distinct `runnerSessionId` UUID, separate `serverRepoRoot` mkdtemp, fresh `tmp` per `beforeEach`. The two postgres-backed canaries can run in either order (or even in parallel under vitest's default thread pool) without cross-contamination.
- **Postgres reuse is principled.** `ensurePostgresStarted()` lazy-boots on first call and reuses the container across canaries ã single ~5s container startup amortized across both Phase D and Phase F.
- **Stub client matches the existing `plugin.test.ts` pattern.** Same `as unknown as Anthropic.Message` cast, same field set (`id/type/role/model/content/stop_reason/stop_sequence/usage`), same nested `usage` shape. The cast is principled given the SDK's discriminated-union types are awkward to construct fully ã the test has chosen to be honest about narrow stubbing rather than performatively thorough.
- **Single-call assertion is load-bearing.** `expect(llmCallCounter.count).toBe(1)` is the I3 (chat:turn-end fires once per turn) witness for the title path. A duplicate-delivery regression elsewhere in the bus would surface here as `count === 2`.
- **`bus.fire` semantics are documented.** Lines 1265ã1269 explicitly note that `bus.fire` awaits subscribers sequentially and that the polling loop is "purely defensive." That's the right framing ã the comment authors know they're belt-and-suspenders. Verified against `hook-bus.ts` line 94: `fire` is `async` and awaits each subscriber's handler in a `for` loop.
- **Strict `!== null` polling matches the spec.** Line 1483: `if (meta.title !== null)`. An empty-string title would not break the loop, would exit at deadline as `null`, and the final `expect(title).toBe('Test Conversation Title')` would surface the bug clearly.
- **CLAUDE.md #3 (no half-wired plugins) is satisfied.** End-to-end chain ã `bus.fire('chat:turn-end')` ã titles subscriber ã `conversations:get` ã `llm:call` ã `conversations:set-title` ã `conversations:get-metadata` ã actually executes (51-test run proves it).
- **Manifest drift safety.** Reuses the existing `PLUGINS_TO_DROP` filter (line 1352) rather than maintaining a parallel list. When the preset adds a new chat-path plugin, this canary picks it up automatically ã same posture as Phase D.

### Issues

No high-confidence (ãË80) issues found.

#### Sub-threshold notes (filtered out, recorded only for context)

These did NOT meet the 80-confidence bar, but listing them so the bar is visible:

- **`@anthropic-ai/sdk` not declared in `presets/k8s/package.json`** ã the test does `import type Anthropic from '@anthropic-ai/sdk'` but only `@ax/llm-anthropic` (which depends on the SDK) is in devDeps. This works under pnpm with hoisting / the existing pnpm-workspace setup, and `pnpm test` confirms it resolves at type-check time. Confidence ~50: this is a type-only import, ergonomic in this monorepo, and the existing pattern doesn't require explicit re-declaration of transitive type-only imports.
- **Stub client omits `container` field** ã SDK 0.91.x's `Anthropic.Message` type may include a `container` field on newer versions; the cast `as unknown as Anthropic.Message` papers over it. Confidence ~40: identical to the pattern in `plugin.test.ts`, and the translator only reads `id/role/type/model/content/stop_reason/usage` (per the comment in `plugin.test.ts` line 8ã9), so missing fields can't surface.
- **Polling loop runs even when `bus.fire` already awaited the subscriber** ã the comment correctly identifies this as defensive. Confidence ~30: documented intent, no actual issue.
- **No assertion that the polled `meta.runnerSessionId` matches what was bound** ã the canary asserts only `title`. Confidence ~25: out of scope for Phase F; Phase D already covers that pathway.

### Final assessment

**Approved.**

Both Task 6 (devDep additions, +2 lines) and Task 7 (canary test, +261 lines) ship a clean, well-isolated end-to-end witness for the Phase F pipeline. The test mirrors its Phase D sibling closely enough that maintenance burden is minimal. The "no half-wired plugins" invariant is satisfied: `@ax/llm-anthropic` and `@ax/conversation-titles` are now reachable from a passing canary.

The only structural choice worth flagging for awareness (not as an issue) is that the canary lives in a `devDependencies`-only test file ã it's never exercised by production preset boot. That's consistent with how the existing Phase D canary is wired (postgres testcontainer + filtered preset, never run in production), and it's the right call given the cost of running an end-to-end k8s + postgres stack in CI for every preset boot. Worth noting only because the Phase F plugins are also currently *not* in the production preset's plugin list ã they're test-only additions. If the intent is for these plugins to ship to production users, that wiring would land in a separate PR (presumably alongside whatever owns the production llm-anthropic API-key sourcing).

docs/plans/2026-04-29-phase-b-pr-notes.md
# Phase B ã PR notes

**Branch:** `feat/phase-b-conversations-metadata`
**Plan:** `docs/plans/2026-04-29-phase-b-conversations-metadata-impl.md`
**Design doc:** `docs/plans/2026-04-29-runner-owned-sessions-design.md`
**Predecessor:** Phase A (spike merged 2026-04-29) ã verified the runner subprocess can redirect HOME so the SDK writes its native session files into the per-conversation workspace. Phase B opens the host-side surface for those files; Phase C closes it.

## What lands

The additive piece of the runner-owned-sessions migration. Four metadata columns + two new service hooks land on `@ax/conversations`. Zero behavior changes for existing callers; the old turns table and old hooks stay alive.

| Slice | Change |
|---|---|
| `migrations.ts` | `ALTER TABLE conversations_v1_conversations ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS runner_type TEXT, runner_session_id TEXT, workspace_ref TEXT, last_activity_at TIMESTAMPTZ` ã all nullable. Pure-additive. Re-runnable. |
| `types.ts` (`Conversation`) | Adds `runnerType`, `runnerSessionId`, `workspaceRef`, `lastActivityAt` (all `string \| null`). |
| `types.ts` (`ConversationsConfig`) | New `defaultRunnerType?: string` knob (default `'claude-sdk'`). Single-runner-per-host MVP (design D5). |
| `store.ts` | New validators: `validateRunnerType`, `validateWorkspaceRefForFreeze`. New methods: `getMetadata` (read-only projection), `storeRunnerSession` (idempotent compare-and-set), `bumpLastActivity` (subscriber-friendly UPDATE). `ConversationStoreCreateArgs` gains optional `runnerType` + `workspaceRef`. |
| `plugin.ts` (`createConversation`) | Captures the resolved agent from `agents:resolve` (no new bus call) and freezes its `workspaceRef` onto the new row. `runner_type` comes from the new config knob. |
| `plugin.ts` (new hooks) | `conversations:get-metadata` (same ACL posture as `:get`). `conversations:store-runner-session` (idempotent first-bind, conflict on mismatch). |
| `plugin.ts` (`chat:turn-end` subscriber) | Bumps `last_activity_at` after a successful `:append-turn`. Heartbeats stay heartbeats. |
| `index.ts` | Public exports: `GetMetadataInput`/`Output`, `StoreRunnerSessionInput`/`Output`, `ConversationMetadata`, `StoreRunnerSessionResult`, `validateRunnerType`, `validateWorkspaceRefForFreeze`. |

Per-package test count: 13 test files / 90 tests, up from 6 / 58 at branch fork. Five new test files: `migrations.test.ts`, `types-shape.test.ts`, `store-phase-b.test.ts`, `create-freezes.test.ts`, `get-metadata.test.ts`, `store-runner-session.test.ts`, `last-activity.test.ts`.

## WINDOW OPEN ã closed by Phase C

Both new service hooks have **no in-process caller** in this PR:

- `conversations:get-metadata` ã Phase C wires the runner plugin's host-side surface, which calls this for sidebar reads (and again per-turn for transcript reads alongside `runner:read-transcript`).
- `conversations:store-runner-session` ã Phase C wires the runner plugin's host-side IPC handler, which receives the runner subprocess's first-turn session-id capture and calls this hook to bind it to the conversation row.

Per memory `feedback_half_wired_window_pattern.md`: this is allowed because Phase C is the next-PR-up. **If Phase C is not the next PR, revert this PR before merging anything else** ã a half-wired window left open across phases drifts into reality, and the columns + hooks here have no value without their callers.

The `chat:turn-end` last-activity bump and `conversations:create` freezing both DO have in-process callers (the existing turn-end subscriber and the live `:create` handler), so those parts are fully wired today.

## Boundary review ã `conversations:get-metadata`

- **Alternate impl this hook could have:** a sqlite-backed `@ax/conversations-sqlite` plugin would register the same hook name with the same input/output shape. Single-replica dev shouldn't need to bring in postgres.
- **Payload field names that might leak:** none. `runnerType`, `runnerSessionId`, `workspaceRef`, `title`, `lastActivityAt`, `createdAt` are all storage-agnostic and runner-agnostic. No `pg_*`, no `sdk_session_id`, no `jsonl_path`.
- **Subscriber risk:** none ã service hook with no subscribers. The eventual sidebar consumer (channel-web in Phase D) will read the projection; the eventual runner-plugin consumer (Phase C) will read `workspaceRef` + `runnerSessionId`.
- **Wire surface (IPC):** none. Host-internal hook only.

## Boundary review ã `conversations:store-runner-session`

- **Alternate impl this hook could have:** a sqlite-backed `@ax/conversations-sqlite` plugin would register the same hook with the same shape. The compare-and-set semantics translate cleanly across SQL backends.
- **Payload field names that might leak:** none. `conversationId` is generic; `runnerSessionId` is opaque (no `sdk_session_id`, no `claude_session_uuid`). The same field name will carry an OpenAI Codex session id when `@ax/agent-codex-cli-runner` ships.
- **Subscriber risk:** none ã service hook with no subscribers. (The runner plugin in Phase C *calls* this hook; it does not subscribe to it.)
- **Wire surface (IPC):** none. Host-internal hook only. The runner subprocess emits a separate IPC event (`event.runner-session-bound`) which the runner plugin's host-side handler translates into this `conversations:*` call.

## Migration safety

- `ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS ...` is idempotent in postgres ãË 9.6. The migration test `re-running does not error` pins this.
- ax-next is greenfield ã no production data to backfill. Existing dev/test conversations get `runner_type = NULL`, `workspace_ref = NULL`, `runner_session_id = NULL`, `last_activity_at = NULL`. Phase C tolerates NULL on read and re-binds on next access.
- `pnpm build && pnpm test` is green. Each of the eight commits leaves the workspace green at its boundary (I13).

## Deviations from design doc

| | Design says | This PR ships | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| **I1** | "v2 side-table, never an in-place ALTER" (existing migration comment) | ALTER `conversations_v1_conversations` in place; no v1ãv2 split, here OR in any future schema change. | The old comment was written for breaking schema splits with production data to preserve. ax-next is greenfield (confirmed with Vinay 2026-04-29). The `_v1` suffix is now a stable identifier, not a version pointer. |

Other phase PR notes (`docs/plans/2026-05-02-runner-owned-sessions-phase-d-impl.md` if a corresponding pr-body exists, `2026-04-28-phase-2-pr-notes.md`, `2026-04-27-phase-1a-pr-notes.md`) follow the same shape.

## Required sections (mirror Phase B's structure)

You are doing a final cumulative review of the entire **Phase F** branch in ax-next. This is the last gate before merge.

**Workspace:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/.worktrees/phase-f-conversation-titles`

7. `55eae5a` ã chore(preset-k8s): add llm-anthropic + conversation-titles as devDeps
8. `c19a9b5` ã test(preset-k8s): canary ã conversation auto-titling via llm:call stub

**Total diff:** 32 files, +2674 lines.

**What this branch ships (Phase F of runner-owned-sessions design):**
- New `conversations:set-title` service hook on `@ax/conversations` (with `ifNull` semantics for atomic compare-and-set).
- New `@ax/llm-anthropic` plugin registering `llm:call` against the Anthropic API.
- New `@ax/conversation-titles` plugin subscribing `chat:turn-end`, calling `llm:call` to generate a title, persisting via `set-title`.
- Canary acceptance test in `presets/k8s/src/__tests__/acceptance.test.ts` exercising the full chain end-to-end.

Each individual task already passed spec review + code quality review. This final review is the holistic check.

## What I want you to look for

This isn't a re-review of every line. It's a check for:

1. **Cross-cutting consistency.** Read-through the full diff. Do the four pieces (set-title hook, llm-anthropic, conversation-titles, canary) tell a coherent story? Any seams that don't match (e.g. inconsistent error codes, inconsistent test patterns, inconsistent JSDoc style)?

2. **CLAUDE.md invariants ã system-level check:**
   - **#1 (hook surface storage-agnostic):** all three new payloads (`set-title`, `llm:call`, `chat:turn-end` consumer) are runner-agnostic / storage-agnostic. Verify by skimming the field names in `LlmCallInput`, `LlmCallOutput`, `SetTitleInput`. (The plan + earlier reviews flagged the Anthropic-shape biases on `LlmCallInput.system` etc. ã these are documented in JSDoc as deferred. Verify the JSDoc is actually present and clear.)
   - **#2 (no cross-plugin imports):** `@ax/conversation-titles` MUST NOT import from `@ax/conversations`. Verify with `grep -rn "from '@ax/conversations'" packages/conversation-titles/src` returning empty.
   - **#3 (no half-wired plugins):** every new hook has a consumer in this PR or in the canary. Verify the canary actually exercises the full chain.
   - **#5 (capabilities minimized):** SECURITY.md files for both new packages are honest about the capability budgets (env, network, FS, spawn, untrusted-input). Re-read both.

3. **Test posture across the branch.**
   - Total new tests: ~64 (96-85=11 set-title + 23 llm-anthropic + 29 conversation-titles + 1 canary).
   - Run `pnpm build && pnpm test` (already verified clean).
   - Are any tests testing mocks instead of code?
   - Are any tests skipped or `it.only`'d?
   - Does the canary test actually fail if you regress the implementation? (Mental test ã if you removed `await bus.call('conversations:set-title')` from `plugin.ts`, the canary's `expect(title).toBe('Test Conversation Title')` would fail.)

4. **PR body sanity:** read `docs/plans/2026-05-03-runner-owned-sessions-phase-f-pr-body.md` (gitignored). Does the PR description honestly reflect what was shipped, including:
   - The half-wired window note (test-only ship; production preset wiring is future).
   - The acknowledged Anthropic-shape biases on `LlmCallInput`.
   - Deviations from the plan (TITLE_MAX 256 not 80, SDK 0.91.1 not 0.30, types mirrored locally not imported).
   - Cumulative test counts.

5. **Discipline check.**
   - YAGNI: anything in this PR that's not required for Phase F? Streaming, tool-use, retitling, smart-quote handling, multimodal ã any of those would be scope creep.
   - DRY: any duplicated logic that should be a helper?
   - One commit per logical change: each commit's diff should match its subject. Check the 8 subjects against the actual diffs.

6. **Boundary review ã final sanity:**
   - `conversations:set-title` payload ã clean.
   - `llm:call` payload ã Anthropic-shape biases acknowledged in code comments; consistent with PR body.
   - `chat:turn-end` consumer ã observation only; doesn't throw.

7. **Documentation quality:**
   - SECURITY.md voice for both new packages: warm, plain, "we", admits complexity. Match the project's CLAUDE.md "Voice & Tone."
   - JSDoc on new public types is informative.
   - Comments explain WHY, not WHAT.

## Run

```bash
git log --oneline 7f08af7..c19a9b5
git diff 7f08af7..c19a9b5 --stat
git diff 7f08af7..c19a9b5
pnpm build
pnpm test
```

## Expected output

A single Markdown report:

**Overall assessment:** Approved / Needs changes.

**Strengths:** what's well-done across the entire branch.

**Issues (severity):**
- **Critical:** any merge-blocking issues.
- **Important:** maintainability / boundary review concerns.
- **Minor:** style, doc, dead code.

**Discipline notes:** any YAGNI / DRY / scope-creep observations.

**Final recommendation:** ship as-is / fix-then-ship / re-architect.

Be honest. If everything is approved, say so plainly. If there's something that should be fixed before merge, name it specifically with file:line references.

1	# Phase F ã PR notes
2	
3	**Branch:** `feat/phase-f-conversation-titles`
4	**Plan:** `docs/plans/2026-05-03-runner-owned-sessions-phase-f-impl.md`
5	**Design doc:** `docs/plans/2026-04-29-runner-owned-sessions-design.md` ôÏD6 + ôÏ"Component responsibilities"
6	**Predecessor:** Phase D (shipped 2026-05-02 as PR #32) ã wired `conversations:get` to read transcripts from the workspace jsonl, which Phase F's title plugin leans on for context.
7	
8	## What lands
9	
10	The last planned slice of the runner-owned-sessions migration. Two new plugins (`@ax/llm-anthropic`, `@ax/conversation-titles`) and one new service hook (`conversations:set-title`) team up to auto-label conversations after the first assistant turn. Existing callers see no behavior change; the title column from Phase B finally has a writer.
11	
12	| Slice | Change |
13	|---|---|
14	| `conversations/src/types.ts` | Adds `SetTitleInput` (with optional `ifNull` flag) and `SetTitleOutput` (`{ updated: boolean }`). |
15	| `conversations/src/store.ts` | New `setTitle()` method. When `ifNull: true`, the UPDATE carries a `WHERE title IS NULL` so re-deliveries can't clobber a user-driven rename. |
16	| `conversations/src/plugin.ts` | Registers `conversations:set-title`, ACL-gated via `agents:resolve` like its siblings. |
17	| `core/src/types.ts` | `LlmCallInput` / `LlmCallOutput` shapes ã provider-neutral on the surface, with a JSDoc note flagging three Anthropic-shape biases (top-level `system`, `messages.role` excludes `'system'`, `content: string` only). Widening earned when a second registrar lands. |
18	| `llm-anthropic/` (new) | Registers `llm:call`. Reads `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` at init (refuses to load without one), one-shot retry on transient 429/5xx, no spawn, no FS. `clientFactory` config option as a test seam. |
19	| `conversation-titles/` (new) | Subscribes to `chat:turn-end`. On the first assistant turn-end where `title IS NULL`, reads the transcript via `conversations:get`, asks Haiku 4.5 for an ãÊ8-word title, validates the output, and writes via `conversations:set-title` with `ifNull: true`. Subscriber posture: never throws. |
20	| `preset-k8s/package.json` | Adds `@ax/llm-anthropic` and `@ax/conversation-titles` as **devDependencies** alongside `@ax/conversations` (which is also still devDeps-only). Production `createK8sPlugins()` is untouched. |
21	| `preset-k8s/src/__tests__/acceptance.test.ts` | New canary `it()` ã boots the preset's chat-path plugin set + a stubbed `@ax/llm-anthropic` (clientFactory returns a fixed string), runs a chat through, polls `conversations:get-metadata` and asserts the title persisted end-to-end. |
22	
23	Per-package test counts: `@ax/conversations` 85 ã 96 (+11). `@ax/llm-anthropic` 0 ã 23 (new). `@ax/conversation-titles` 0 ã 29 (new). `@ax/preset-k8s` 50 ã 51 (+1 canary). Net new: ~64 tests.
24	
25	## Half-wired window ã test-only ship, NOT an I3 violation
26	
27	Worth being precise about: **neither new plugin loads in production `createK8sPlugins()`.** Both are devDeps + canary only.
28	
29	Here's the chain:
30	
31	- `@ax/llm-anthropic` registers `llm:call`. Its consumer is `@ax/conversation-titles`, which lands in the same PR.
32	- `@ax/conversation-titles` calls `llm:call`, `conversations:get`, and `conversations:set-title`. All three are reachable in the canary in this PR.
33	- The canary acceptance test (`presets/k8s/src/__tests__/acceptance.test.ts` Phase F `it()`) wires all three together with a stubbed Anthropic client and asserts end-to-end behavior. CLAUDE.md #3's "reachable from the canary acceptance test" requirement is met.
34	
35	The reason these don't go into production `createK8sPlugins()` is upstream: **`@ax/conversations` itself isn't loaded there yet.** `@ax/channel-web` (the eventual sidebar reader) isn't either. The whole conversations + channel-web + Phase F triple ships together when someone wires that production path. Loading Phase F into production today would break boot ã `@ax/conversation-titles` calls `conversations:get`, which has no registrar in production yet.
36	
37	So this is a **test-only ship awaiting prerequisites**, not the I3 "wire-this-up-later" trap. The plugins are fully implemented, fully tested, and reachable from the canary. They're not half-wired in the I3 sense ã there's no host-internal infrastructure here that's "ready but unwired." Everything is wired; it's the consumer environment that hasn't been assembled yet.
38	
39	When a future PR lands `@ax/conversations` + `@ax/channel-web` in production, that same PR should add `@ax/llm-anthropic` + `@ax/conversation-titles` to the production plugin list.
40	
41	## Boundary review ã `conversations:set-title`
42	
43	- **Alternate impl this hook could have:** a sqlite-backed `@ax/conversations-sqlite` would register the same hook with the same shape. Single-replica dev shouldn't pull in postgres. The `ifNull` semantics translate cleanly across SQL backends.
44	- **Payload field names that might leak:** none. `conversationId`, `userId`, `title`, `ifNull` are all generic and storage-agnostic. No `pg_*`, no row IDs, no `update_clause`.
45	- **Subscriber risk:** none ã service hook with no subscribers. `@ax/conversation-titles` *calls* the hook; it does not subscribe to it.
46	- **Wire surface (IPC):** none. Host-internal hook only.
47	
48	## Boundary review ã `llm:call`
49	
50	- **Alternate impl this hook could have:** an OpenAI-shaped registrar (`@ax/llm-openai`), a local Ollama registrar, or a Bedrock registrar. The shape is provider-neutral on the surface ã `model`, `messages`, `maxTokens`, `temperature`, `system`.
51	- **Payload field names that might leak:** the canonical `LlmCallInput` carries three Anthropic-shaped biases that we've documented in `packages/core/src/types.ts` JSDoc rather than papered over:
52	  1. Top-level `system` field (OpenAI puts the system prompt as a `messages[0]` with `role: 'system'`).
53	  2. `messages[].role` excludes `'system'` (OpenAI allows it).
54	  3. `content` is `string` only (Anthropic & OpenAI both support content arrays for multimodal; we don't yet).
55	  These are flagged as "if a second registrar bites, widen the canonical shape before a third one shows up." Phase F is the only consumer today, so the cost of a future widen is bounded.
56	  `stopReason` is normalized to a closed union (`end_turn` / `max_tokens` / `tool_use` / `stop_sequence` / `unknown`); provider-specific values collapse to `'unknown'`.
57	- **Subscriber risk:** none ã service hook with one registrar.
58	- **Wire surface (IPC):** none. Host-internal hook only.
59	
60	## Boundary review ã `chat:turn-end` subscriber
61	
62	- **Posture:** observation only. The subscriber's outer try/catch wraps the entire handler; a thrown error logs `conversation_titles_subscriber_failed` at warn and returns. The bus's at-least-once delivery is safe because `ifNull: true` on `set-title` guards against double-writes.
63	- **Payload reads:** defensive. We read `payload.role` (the only field we care about) and re-fetch the canonical transcript via `conversations:get` rather than trusting whatever the publisher attached. If the payload shape drifts, the subscriber no-ops on `role !== 'assistant'` instead of crashing.
64	
65	## Capability minimization (CLAUDE.md #5)
66	
67	| Plugin | env | network | filesystem | spawn | untrusted input |
68	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
69	| `@ax/llm-anthropic` | reads `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` at `init()` only; refuses to load without one | outbound HTTPS to `api.anthropic.com` only | none | none | caller-provided `messages` content passed to Anthropic as opaque text ã no parser, no eval |
70	| `@ax/conversation-titles` | none | none | none | none | LLM output validated by `validateGeneratedTitle()` (strip outer quotes, first line only, length cap, "Untitled" ã null) before persist |
71	
72	Both new plugins ship `SECURITY.md` walking the three-threat-model checklist. `@ax/conversation-titles` has zero external surface ã bus calls only.
73	
74	## Deviations from design / plan
75	
76	| | Plan / design says | This PR ships | Why |
77	|---|---|---|---|
78	| **D1** | `TITLE_MAX = 80` (mirrored in `validate.ts`) | `TITLE_MAX = 256` in both `packages/conversations/src/store.ts` and `packages/conversation-titles/src/validate.ts` | Plan was written before re-reading the existing constant. 256 has been the storage cap since Phase B's `validateTitle`; mirroring 80 would cause `validateGeneratedTitle()` to truncate strings the store would happily accept. |
79	| **D2** | `@anthropic-ai/sdk: ^0.30.0` | `@anthropic-ai/sdk: ^0.91.1` | The workspace's `pnpm.overrides` forces ãË0.91.1 for security (CVE patches). Downgrading would have failed the override check. The 0.30 in the plan was copied from the runner's older pin ã out of date. |
80	| **D3** | `@ax/conversation-titles` imports types from `@ax/conversations` | Types mirrored locally in `packages/conversation-titles/src/types.ts` with "Mirrored from ãÎ keep in lockstep" comments. | I2 (no cross-plugin imports) doesn't carve out a types-only exception in the eslint allowlist. Precedent: `packages/conversations/src/store.ts` already mirrors `@ax/agents`'s `WORKSPACE_REF_RE` rather than importing it. Drift is caught at code-review time + at runtime via the bus payload contract. |
81	| **D4** | Load both plugins in `createK8sPlugins()`; canary boots them via the production preset | Plugins live in `presets/k8s/devDependencies` only; canary instantiates them inline | `@ax/conversations` itself isn't loaded in production `createK8sPlugins()` yet (channel-web isn't either). Loading Phase F into production today would crash on boot ã `@ax/conversation-titles` calls `conversations:get`, which has no registrar there. The right wiring is "all four together in a future PR" ã see the half-wired-window section above. |
82	
83	## Test summary
84	
85	- `@ax/conversations`: 96 tests (was 85, +11 in `set-title.test.ts` covering ACL, ifNull semantics, idempotency, length validation, missing/soft-deleted rows, and the `agents:resolve` gate).
86	- `@ax/llm-anthropic`: 23 tests across `translate.test.ts` (13 ã request/response shape conversions, multi-block concat, edge cases) and `plugin.test.ts` (10 ã init failure, registration, retry policy, error mapping).
87	- `@ax/conversation-titles`: 29 tests across `prompt.test.ts` (8 ã flattening, transcript budget, thinking-block drop, tool-block shorthand), `validate.test.ts` (10 ã trim, quote strip, "Untitled" rejection, length cap, newline handling), and `plugin.test.ts` (11 ã full subscriber matrix including swallow-on-LLM-fail, ifNull race, validation null path).
88	- `@ax/preset-k8s`: 51 tests (was 50). The new Phase F `it()` boots the chat-path plugin set with a stubbed Anthropic client and asserts the title persists end-to-end.
89	- Net new tests: ~64.
90	
91	`pnpm build` clean. `pnpm test` clean repo-wide. Each of the eight commits leaves the workspace green at its boundary.
92	
93	## Sequencing implication for next phase
94	
95	**This is the last planned phase of the runner-owned-sessions migration.** With Phase F merged, the design at `docs/plans/2026-04-29-runner-owned-sessions-design.md` is fully implemented:
96	
97	- Phase A ã HOME-redirect spike (shipped 2026-04-29).
98	- Phase B ã additive metadata columns + hooks (shipped 2026-04-29 as PR #29).
99	- Phase C ã runner-host scaffolding (parked, subsumed by 2026-05-01 workspace redesign).
100	- Phase D ã `conversations:get` reads from workspace jsonl (shipped 2026-05-02 as PR #32).
101	- Phase E ã runner-owned sessions (shipped, see PR #35).
102	- Phase F ã auto-titling (this PR).
103	
104	Future work is tracked separately:
105	
106	- **Production preset wiring.** A follow-up PR moves `@ax/conversations`, `@ax/channel-web`, `@ax/llm-anthropic`, and `@ax/conversation-titles` from devDeps into `createK8sPlugins()` together. None can land alone ã the dependency chain has to clear all at once.
107	- **`:list` order-by `last_activity_at DESC`.** Phase B left this hanging; Phase F doesn't need it. A small follow-up can flip the default ordering once enough rows have non-null `last_activity_at` to make the change safe.
108	- **Manual user re-titling.** `set-title` already supports unconditional overwrite (`ifNull: false` is the default). UI surface for that is a channel-web concern, not a Phase F one.
109	
110	## Reviewer asks
111	
112	1. **Boundary review on `LlmCallInput` biases.** The three Anthropic-shape biases are flagged in the JSDoc. Confirm the "widen when a second registrar bites" posture is the right tradeoff vs. paying the abstraction cost now.
113	2. **Test-only ship framing.** Confirm the half-wired-window section's framing ã that this is "awaiting prerequisites" not "wire-it-later" ã matches your read of CLAUDE.md #3 + I3.
114	3. **Local type mirroring.** Confirm the `Conversation` / `Turn` / `GetInput` / `SetTitleInput` mirror in `packages/conversation-titles/src/types.ts` is the right shape (we declare only the fields we read; runtime drift on unread fields is invisible to us, which is intentional).
115	4. **Deviations D1/D2/D3/D4.** Plan-vs-reality drift. None of these change the user-facing behavior; they're shape and packaging decisions made during implementation. Confirm rationale.
116	
117	## Follow-ups (don't block this PR)
118	
119	- **Production preset wiring** for `@ax/conversations` + `@ax/channel-web` + Phase F plugins (separate PR; none can ship in isolation ã see above).
120	- **OpenAI / local-model `llm:call` registrar.** Will earn the `LlmCallInput` widen ã top-level `system` ã `messages[0].role: 'system'`, `content: string` ã `content: string | Array<...>`. Defer until a real second consumer exists.
121	- **Re-titling UX.** Channel-web feature, not Phase F.
122

| `preset-k8s/src/__tests__/acceptance.test.ts` | New canary `it()` ã boots the preset's chat-path plugin set + a stubbed `@ax/llm-anthropic` (clientFactory returns a fixed string), runs a chat through, polls `conversations:get-metadata` and asserts the title persisted end-to-end. |

- `@ax/conversation-titles` calls `llm:call`, `conversations:get`, and `conversations:set-title`. All three are reachable in the canary in this PR.
- The canary acceptance test (`presets/k8s/src/__tests__/acceptance.test.ts` Phase F `it()`) wires all three together with a stubbed Anthropic client and asserts end-to-end behavior. CLAUDE.md #3's "reachable from the canary acceptance test" requirement is met.

| `@ax/conversation-titles` | none | none | none | none | LLM output validated by `validateGeneratedTitle()` (strip outer quotes, first line only, length cap, "Untitled" ã null) before persist |

Both new plugins ship `SECURITY.md` walking the three-threat-model checklist. `@ax/conversation-titles` has zero external surface ã bus calls only.

| | Plan / design says | This PR ships | Why |

The right wiring is "all four together in a future PR" ã see the half-wired-window section above. |

## Test summary

- `@ax/conversations`: 96 tests (was 85, +11 in `set-title.test.ts` covering ACL, ifNull semantics, idempotency, length validation, missing/soft-deleted rows, and the `agents:resolve` gate).
- `@ax/llm-anthropic`: 23 tests across `translate.test.ts` (13 ã request/response shape conversions, multi-block concat, edge cases) and `plugin.test.ts` (10 ã init failure, registration, retry policy, error mapping).
- `@ax/conversation-titles`: 29 tests across `prompt.test.ts` (8 ã flattening, transcript budget, thinking-block drop, tool-block shorthand), `validate.test.ts` (10 ã trim, quote strip, "Untitled" rejection, length cap, newline handling), and `plugin.test.ts` (11 ã full subscriber matrix including swallow-on-LLM-fail, ifNull race, validation null path).
- `@ax/preset-k8s`: 51 tests (was 50). The new Phase F `it()` boots the chat-path plugin set with a stubbed Anthropic client and asserts the title persists end-to-end.
- Net new tests: ~64.

`pnpm build` clean. `pnpm test` clean repo-wide. Each of the eight commits leaves the workspace green at its boundary.

## Sequencing implication for next phase

**This is the last planned phase of the runner-owned-sessions migration.** With Phase F merged, the design at `docs/plans/2026-04-29-runner-owned-sessions-design.md` is fully implemented:

- Phase A ã HOME-redirect spike (shipped 2026-04-29).
- Phase B ã additive metadata columns + hooks (shipped 2026-04-29 as PR #29).
- Phase C ã runner-host scaffolding (parked, subsumed by 2026-05-01 workspace redesign).
- Phase D ã `conversations:get` reads from workspace jsonl (shipped 2026-05-02 as PR #32).
- Phase E ã runner-owned sessions (shipped, see PR #35).
- Phase F ã auto-titling (this PR).

Future work is tracked separately:

- **Production preset wiring.** A follow-up PR moves `@ax/conversations`, `@ax/channel-web`, `@ax/llm-anthropic`, and `@ax/conversation-titles` from devDeps into `createK8sPlugins()` together. None can land alone ã the dependency chain has to clear all at once.
- **`:list` order-by `last_activity_at DESC`.** Phase B left this hanging; Phase F doesn't need it. A small follow-up can flip the default ordering once enough rows have non-null `last_activity_at` to make the change safe.
- **Manual user re-titling.** `set-title` already supports unconditional overwrite (`ifNull: false` is the default). UI surface for that is a channel-web concern, not a Phase F one.

## Reviewer asks

1. **Boundary review on `LlmCallInput` biases.** The three Anthropic-shape biases are flagged in the JSDoc. Confirm the "widen when a second registrar bites" posture is the right tradeoff vs. paying the abstraction cost now.
2. **Test-only ship framing.** Confirm the half-wired-window section's framing ã that this is "awaiting prerequisites" not "wire-it-later" ã matches your read of CLAUDE.md #3 + I3.
3. **Local type mirroring.** Confirm the `Conversation` / `Turn` / `GetInput` / `SetTitleInput` mirror in `packages/conversation-titles/src/types.ts` is the right shape (we declare only the fields we read; runtime drift on unread fields is invisible to us, which is intentional).
4. **Deviations D1/D2/D3/D4.** Plan-vs-reality drift. None of these change the user-facing behavior; they're shape and packaging decisions made during implementation. Confirm rationale.

## Follow-ups (don't block this PR)

- **Production preset wiring** for `@ax/conversations` + `@ax/channel-web` + Phase F plugins (separate PR; none can ship in isolation ã see above).
- **OpenAI / local-model `llm:call` registrar.** Will earn the `LlmCallInput` widen ã top-level `system` ã `messages[0].role: 'system'`, `content: string` ã `content: string | Array<...>`. Defer until a real second consumer exists.
- **Re-titling UX.** Channel-web feature, not Phase F.

content.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code when working with code in this repository.

## Repository

- **Name:** ax-next ã AX v2, greenfield rewrite
- **Shape:** Monorepo (pnpm + changesets + tsconfig refs). Each package is a plugin; `@ax/core` is the kernel. Scaffolding is Week 1 of build ã not yet wired up.
- **Architecture spec:** `docs/plans/2026-04-22-plugin-architecture-design.md`
- **Conventions spec:** `docs/plans/2026-04-23-v2-claude-md-and-skills.md`

## Legacy reference

The v1 AX codebase lives at `~/dev/ai/ax/`. It is a **read-only reference** ã consult it for edge cases and port specific helpers (e.g., `safePath`, canary scanner, k8s pod lifecycle bits from the correlation-ID work), but:

- Do NOT mutate it from this repo.
- Do NOT carry over its orchestration shape. The expensive parts of v1 (`server-completions.ts`, the IPC handler dispatcher, the multi-mode sandbox manager) are exactly what v2 removes. Porting them is wasted work.
- Do NOT port v1's `.claude/skills/ax/*` sub-skills. They encode v1 architecture that's being replaced. v2 skills are friction-driven (see Skills, below).

## Build / Test

```bash
pnpm build
pnpm test
pnpm test --filter @ax/<plugin>
```

(Tooling lands in Week 1ã2 per architecture doc Section 10.)

## The five invariants (read before touching code)

1. **Hook surface is transport-agnostic and storage-agnostic.** No git/sqlite/k8s vocabulary in hook payloads. If a payload field name only makes sense for one backend, it leaks. (See workspace abstraction ã architecture doc Section 4.5.)

2. **No cross-plugin imports.** Plugins talk through the hook bus only. Will be enforced by lint (`eslint.config.mjs` `no-restricted-imports`) once scaffolded. The hook bus IS the inter-plugin API.

3. **No half-wired plugins.** A plugin is either fully registered + tested + reachable from the canary acceptance test, or it doesn't merge. No "wire this up later" PRs.

4. **One source of truth per concept.** If two plugins both store state about the same thing (skills, tools, sessions), one of them is wrong. Coordinate through service hooks, not shared rows.

5. **Capabilities are explicit and minimized.** Every plugin, tool, IPC handler, and sandbox boundary grants the smallest set of capabilities it needs ã no more. The list to think about: filesystem paths, network reach, process spawn, env access, untrusted-input handling.

   Untrusted content (model output, tool output, user input crossing a trust boundary, third-party plugin code) is treated as untrusted at every hop. The whole point of v2 over openclaw is that we're the secure one ã if a hook surface, IPC action, or plugin grants more reach than it strictly requires, that's the bug.

   When touching sandbox boundaries, IPC transport, plugin loading, or any code path that handles untrusted content, invoke the `security-checklist` skill.

## Boundary review (required for new hooks)

When adding or changing a service-hook signature, or adding a subscriber hook with a non-trivial payload, answer in the PR description:

- **Alternate impl this hook could have:** name one. (If you can't, consider whether this needs to be a hook at all vs. just a function inside one plugin.)
- **Payload field names that might leak:** list any, or "none". (e.g., `sha`, `bucket`, `pod_name`, `socket_path`. If present, justify or rename.)
- **Subscriber risk:** could a subscriber key off a backend-specific field and break when the alternate impl ships?
- **Wire surface (if this is also an IPC action):** schema lives in this plugin's directory, not a central file.

If you can't name an alternate impl, the abstraction may be premature ã just write a function. If field names leak, rename now (cheap) before subscribers depend on them (expensive).

Patches that only change a plugin's **internal** implementation (no hook-surface change) don't need boundary review.

## Half-Wired Code Policy

If you write infrastructure (IPC transport, sandbox provider, bridge, etc.) that's not actually called by the running system, either wire it in within the same PR or don't merge it. "We'll wire it later" code becomes a trap ã it confuses readers, drifts from the rest of the system, and represents work that looks done but isn't.

## Bug Fix Policy

Whenever you fix a bug that wasn't caught by an existing test, you MUST add a test that would have caught it the first time. No exception. The test goes in before the fix is considered done.

## Skills

v2 is friction-driven. A skill gets written only when:

1. 3+ sessions have hit the same friction in the same area, AND
2. The friction is non-obvious from reading the code.

Skills are NOT written for documentation of what the code does, conventions that aren't yet established, or speculation about what might be useful later. Test: a skill should answer "I tried X and it didn't work ã what's the trick?"

Day-1 skills:

- `ax-conventions` ã the five invariants, plugin manifest format, hook bus mechanics, boundary-review checklist.
- `claude-memory` ã per-project working memory in `.claude/memory/` (gitignored). Captures project facts, decisions, patterns, mistakes, and self-observations across sessions. See `docs/plans/2026-04-23-claude-memory-skill-design.md`.
- `security-checklist` ã three-threat-model walk (sandbox escape, prompt injection, supply chain) producing a structured PR security note. Fires on sandbox / IPC / plugin loading / untrusted content / new-dependency changes. See `docs/plans/2026-04-23-security-checklist-skill-design.md`.

Everything else is deferred until earned.

## Voice & Tone for User-Facing Content

When generating or editing any user-facing content (README, SECURITY.md, docs, error messages, CLI output, comments visible to users), use the following voice:

### Personality

- **Self-deprecating but competent.** We joke about our paranoia, not about security itself. We're the friend who triple-checks the door is locked and laughs about it ã but the door IS locked.
- **Warm and approachable.** Assume the reader is smart but not necessarily technical. Never gatekeep. Never make someone feel dumb for not knowing what a CVE is.
- **Honest about complexity.** Security is hard. We say so. We don't pretend things are simple when they aren't, but we do our best to make them understandable.
- **Sarcastic toward bad practices, never toward people.** We roast hardcoded API keys, not the person who committed them. We've all been there.

### Writing guidelines

- Plain language first, jargon second. If you use a technical term, briefly explain it or link to an explanation.
- Short sentences. Short paragraphs. Walls of text are a security vulnerability for attention spans.
- Funny is fine. Funny instead of clear is not.
- When discussing real vulnerabilities, threats, or security configurations, drop the jokes and be direct. Lives and livelihoods depend on this stuff.
- Default to "we" not "you" ã we're on the same team as the reader.
- Admit what we don't know. Uncertainty stated clearly is more trustworthy than false confidence.

### Examples

**Good:** "We pin our dependencies because we have trust issues. But also because unpinned dependencies are how supply chain attacks happen, and we'd rather be paranoid than compromised."

**Good:** "This step is optional but recommended. Kind of like locking your car in a safe neighborhood ã probably fine if you skip it, but you'll feel better if you don't."

**Bad:** "If you don't understand why this matters, you probably shouldn't be deploying to production."

**Bad:** "Simply configure your TLS mutual authentication with certificate pinning." (nothing about this is "simply")

### Golden rule

We're a nervous crab peeking through its claws ã but behind those claws, we know exactly what we're doing.

input.command:
gh pr create --title "feat: Phase F ã auto-titling via @ax/llm-anthropic + @ax/conversation-titles" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

Closes Phase F of the runner-owned-sessions design (`docs/plans/2026-04-29-runner-owned-sessions-design.md` ôÏD6). Conversations now auto-title themselves after the first assistant turn lands.

- New `conversations:set-title` service hook on `@ax/conversations` with `ifNull` semantics for atomic compare-and-set (safe under at-least-once delivery).
- New `@ax/llm-anthropic` plugin registers `llm:call` against the Anthropic API. Reads `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` at init, retries once on 429/5xx, exposes a `clientFactory` test seam.
- New `@ax/conversation-titles` plugin subscribes `chat:turn-end`, reads transcript via `conversations:get`, asks Haiku 4.5 for an ãÊ8-word title, validates, persists via `set-title`.
- Canary acceptance test in `presets/k8s/src/__tests__/acceptance.test.ts` exercises the full chain end-to-end with a stubbed Anthropic client.

8 commits, 32 files, +2674 lines. Net new tests: ~64 (96 in conversations, 23 in llm-anthropic, 29 in conversation-titles, +1 canary).

## Half-wired window ã test-only ship, NOT an I3 violation

Both new plugins are devDeps + canary only ã they do **not** load in production `createK8sPlugins()`. That's because `@ax/conversations` itself isn't loaded there yet (channel-web isn't either); loading Phase F into production today would crash on boot. Everything is fully implemented, fully tested, and reachable from the canary. A future PR wires `@ax/conversations` + `@ax/channel-web` + Phase F plugins into production together ã none can ship in isolation.

Full PR notes (deviations, boundary review, capability table, sequencing) at `docs/plans/2026-05-03-runner-owned-sessions-phase-f-pr-body.md` (gitignored locally).

## Boundary review

- **`conversations:set-title`** ã alternate impl: any metadata store (sqlite, kv). Payload (`conversationId`, `userId`, `title`, `ifNull`) all generic. No subscribers; no IPC.
- **`llm:call`** ã alternate impl: OpenAI / local model / Bedrock. Three Anthropic-shape biases (top-level `system`, `messages.role` excludes 'system', `content: string` only) flagged in JSDoc as deferred ã widening earned when a second registrar lands. `stopReason` collapses provider-specific values to `'unknown'`.
- **`chat:turn-end` subscriber** ã observation only, never throws (try/catch + log + swallow). Defensive payload reads (`role` only); re-fetches canonical transcript via `conversations:get` rather than trusting publisher attachments.

## Deviations from plan

| | Plan said | Ships | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| D1 | `TITLE_MAX = 80` | 256 | Plan was wrong; existing `validateTitle` in store has been 256 since Phase B. |
| D2 | `@anthropic-ai/sdk: ^0.30.0` | `^0.91.1` | Workspace `pnpm.overrides` forces ãË0.91.1 for security. |
| D3 | Import types from `@ax/conversations` | Mirror locally | I2 (no cross-plugin imports) ã same precedent as `store.ts` mirroring `@ax/agents` validators. |
| D4 | Load both plugins in `createK8sPlugins()` | devDeps + canary only | Conversations isn't loaded in production preset; loading Phase F there would break boot. |

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm build` ã clean repo-wide.
- [x] `pnpm test` ã clean repo-wide.
- [x] `pnpm --filter @ax/conversations test` ã 96/96 (+11 set-title cases).
- [x] `pnpm --filter @ax/llm-anthropic test` ã 23/23 (translate + plugin retry/init).
- [x] `pnpm --filter @ax/conversation-titles test` ã 29/29 (prompt + validate + subscriber matrix).
- [x] `pnpm --filter @ax/preset-k8s test` ã 51/51 (Phase F canary asserts title persists end-to-end + LLM called exactly once).
- [ ] Manual canary in kind with a real `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (deferred ã production preset wiring is a separate PR per the half-wired window section above).

ÞÊ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"

**Actionable comments posted: 3**
ÞÏ¿ Nitpick comments (3)
packages/conversation-titles/src/validate.ts (1)
`26-41`: _ÞÊ Low value_ **Solid trust-boundary sanitizer; one optional hardening note.** The quote-stripping, first-line extraction, length truncation, and empty/sentinel checks all look correct. The `s.length >= 2` guard on the quote-strip branch is technically redundant (a 1-char `"` without it would yield `''` ã `null` anyway) but harmless. One low-priority observation: the `'Untitled'` sentinel check (Line 38) is case-sensitive. A misbehaving model returning `"untitled"` or `"UNTITLED"` would slip through. Given the prompt instructs ãÊ8-word concise titles and this is already a defensive path, the risk is very low ã but if the prompt ever returns other casing variants it's worth tightening:
ÞÀÿ¡ Optional case-insensitive sentinel ```diff - if (s === 'Untitled') return null; + if (s.toLowerCase() === 'untitled') return null; ```
ÞÊ Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@packages/conversation-titles/src/validate.ts` around lines 26 - 41, The validateGeneratedTitle function currently treats the 'Untitled' sentinel case-sensitively; update the sentinel check in validateGeneratedTitle so it compares case-insensitively (e.g., compare s.toLowerCase() to 'untitled') before returning null, leaving the existing quote-stripping, first-line extraction, length truncation (TITLE_MAX) and empty checks intact. ```
packages/conversation-titles/src/__tests__/validate.test.ts (1)
`25-26`: _ãÀ Quick win_ **Add a quoted-`Untitled` regression case.** This suite proves quote stripping and sentinel rejection separately, but not the combined path a model is likely to return (`"Untitled"`). If `validateGeneratedTitle()` checks the sentinel before removing outer quotes, these tests still pass while the plugin persists the fallback title.
Suggested test addition ```diff it('returns null on the literal "Untitled" sentinel', () => { expect(validateGeneratedTitle('Untitled')).toBeNull(); }); + + it('returns null when the Untitled sentinel is wrapped in quotes', () => { + expect(validateGeneratedTitle('"Untitled"')).toBeNull(); + }); ```
ÞÊ Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@packages/conversation-titles/src/__tests__/validate.test.ts` around lines 25 - 26, The test suite lacks a regression case for the model returning the sentinel wrapped in quotes; add a test in packages/conversation-titles/src/__tests__/validate.test.ts that calls validateGeneratedTitle('"Untitled"') (i.e., the string including surrounding quotes) and asserts the result is null so we verify quote-stripping and sentinel rejection are applied in the combined path; ensure the new test uses the same helper/describe context and the validateGeneratedTitle symbol so it fails if the function checks the sentinel before trimming quotes. ```
packages/llm-anthropic/src/plugin.ts (1)
`14-36`: _ãÀ Quick win_ **Consider exposing a `timeoutMs` config option** The Anthropic SDK's default request timeout is 600 s. `LlmAnthropicConfig` has no first-class timeout option ã `clientFactory` covers the test seam, but production callers have no way to tighten the timeout without providing a fully wired client. If `llm:call` is ever invoked on a request path (not only from the fire-and-forget `chat:turn-end` subscriber), a slow Anthropic API response will block the caller for up to 10 minutes.
ã£ÿ¡ Proposed addition ```diff export interface LlmAnthropicConfig { apiKey?: string; defaultModel?: string; defaultMaxTokens?: number; + /** + * HTTP request timeout in milliseconds passed to the Anthropic client. + * Defaults to the SDK default (600 000 ms). ÞÊ Prompt for AI Agents ``` Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed. In `@packages/llm-anthropic/src/plugin.ts` around lines 14 - 36, Add a timeoutMs?: number field to LlmAnthropicConfig (with a brief doc comment like "request timeout in milliseconds; defaults to SDK default") and wire it into the Anthropic client creation in init: when using clientFactory or constructing the real Anthropic client, pass the configured timeoutMs (or omit if undefined) so production callers can override the SDK's long default; reference LlmAnthropicConfig, timeoutMs, clientFactory and init in your change. ```

aiTitle: Create a plan for phase F

content.text:
CodeRabbit posted 3 major and 3 nit comments. Triage:

| # | Comment | Action |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | plugin.ts:125 ã gate to first assistant turn only | Fix (correctness) |
| 2 | plugin.ts:136 ã stop logging raw `llmOut.text` | Fix (security) |
| 3 | plugin.ts:544 ã distinguish delete race from `ifNull` miss | Fix (correctness) |
| 4 | validate.ts ã case-insensitive 'Untitled' | Fix (cheap) |
| 5 | validate.test ã add `'"Untitled"'` regression test | Fix (cheap) |
| 6 | llm-anthropic ã `timeoutMs` config option | Fix (small surface) |

Dispatching implementer.
